resources.string: Permission needed — tap the button above.
Review available — freeze still active. Confirm to change.
Reboot detected — freeze stays LOCKED until real time is verified from the network.\nConnect to the internet to verify. (Extending is still allowed.)
Enter a positive number of minutes.
Or start a manual freeze (does not advance the taper):
Cycle complete! Freeze tier is now %1$d day(s).
Built-in test domain — cannot be removed.
This page is blocked during your freeze.

Reads the address bar of browsers to block pages on your blocklist and enforce safe-search, and detects specific in-app surfaces (Instagram Reels/Stories, YouTube Shorts) to block them — but only while a freeze is running. No browsing data is stored or sent anywhere.

This feature uses Android's Accessibility service to read the URL shown in your browser's address bar.

What it does with that text:
• Compares the current URL against your local blocklist (shared with the VPN feature).
• If it matches, it navigates the browser away from the page and shows a brief "Blocked" notice.
• For Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo searches it enforces safe-search, and blocks searches containing banned keywords.
• In Instagram and YouTube it detects specific surfaces you've chosen to block (Reels, Stories, Shorts) and navigates away — the rest of those apps stays usable.

Important guarantees:
• It only reads the address bar of browser apps, and only while a freeze is running.
• It does NOT log, store, or transmit your browsing history — matching happens entirely on-device.
• This is a separate, optional module. You can turn it off any time in Accessibility settings without affecting the rest of the app.
Status: not enabled — tap below to turn it on.
Updated to %1$d domains. %2$d source(s) ok; kept last-known for: %3$s
Firebase is not configured. Add google-services.json to enable sign-in. The app\'s protection features work without it.
Enter an email and a password of at least 6 characters.
Account created. A verification link was sent to %1$s — tap it to verify your email.
Google sign-in isn\'t configured yet. Enable the Google provider in Firebase and add the SHA-1, then rebuild.
Email: not verified — check your inbox for the verification link.
Contact info only — not used for login or verification.
Type the numbers 1 to 50 in order with no spaces or commas (123456789101112…4950). You must type each character by hand — pasting or bulk input is rejected.
Paste / bulk input is not allowed — type each digit by hand.
That doesn\'t match the sequence — check the last digit.
Correct! Confirmed by the server and logged.
Correct! (Sign in to record this on the server.)

Fliptle helps you stay off distracting and adult content. You set a freeze; while it runs, the apps and websites you chose stay blocked. A freeze can\'t be shortened once it starts — only extended. As you complete freezes, the taper lengthens the next one, up to 7 days.\n\nNext we\'ll sign you in (optional) and turn on a few permissions the blocking needs. We\'ll explain each one.
Phone sign-in lets Fliptle recognise your account across reinstalls. It\'s optional — all protection works without it, and nothing about your browsing is tied to your number.
Fliptle needs Usage Access to see which app is currently in the foreground, so it can cover apps you\'ve chosen to block during a freeze. It only reads the name of the current app — nothing else.
This lets Fliptle show a full-screen \"blocked\" cover on top of a blocked app or website. Without it, blocking can\'t cover the screen.

Fliptle runs a private, on-device VPN that filters DNS to block adult websites. It has NO remote server and does not route your traffic anywhere — it only checks the domain names your device looks up against the blocklist. You can turn it off any time.

Fliptle uses Android's Accessibility service to read the web address shown in your browser's address bar, so it can block sites on the list and enforce safe-search — but only while a freeze is running.

What it does with that text:
• Compares the current URL to the on-device blocklist.
• If it matches, it leaves the page and shows a brief notice.

It also detects specific in-app surfaces you choose to block — Instagram Reels/Stories and YouTube Shorts — and navigates away from them, leaving the rest of those apps usable.

What it never does:
• It does NOT record, store, or transmit your browsing history or any page content. Every check happens on your device.

This step is optional, and you can turn it off any time in Accessibility settings.
Locked — verifying real time after reboot
Blocked apps: %1$d • Adult domains: %2$d\nWebsite filter: %3$s • URL blocking: %4$s
Review available — blocks still active. Confirm to change.
Fliptle can\'t protect you right now because a required permission is turned off. Re-enable it below to continue. The app stays locked until protection is restored.
You can leave this screen with Back or Home at any time — your phone stays fully usable for calls and emergencies. Fliptle will keep reminding you until protection is back on.
Block specific distraction surfaces inside apps while a freeze is running. Each is independent — the rest of Instagram and YouTube stays usable. Requires the Accessibility service to be on.

Note: these surfaces are detected from Instagram\'s and YouTube\'s internal view IDs, which are not a public API and may break when those apps update. Only the full-screen player is blocked — the feed, DMs, profile, search and normal videos stay usable.
With debug on, nothing is blocked; the app records the view IDs it matches so you can see exactly what triggers a block. Open Instagram/YouTube, browse around, then come back and Refresh.
No matches logged yet. Turn on debug, open Instagram/YouTube, then Refresh.
